notes: Tested whether the engineering layer contradicts, weakens, or inverts. (1) Does it weaken mahā-rathāḥ? No — preserved as 'technically competent peers' which carries the competent-witness restriction. (2) Does it invert the bhayāt-default? No — preserved as 'the peer-default for why does a senior leave a system mid-cutover is fear of finishing it.' (3) Does it import 'vanity-management' connotation? Explicitly no — falsifiability and counter_example exclude personal-branding. (4) Does it drop the bahu-mataḥ-to-lāghavam phase-change? No — explicitly preserved. Reverse-translate: a Sanskrit reader reading only the engineering layer would reconstruct a stance compatible with mahā-rathāḥ + bhayāt + bahu-mataḥ + lāghavam — the competent-witness, the fear-default, the prior-standing-to-becoming-small phase change are all reconstructable. PASS on both 3.6 and 7.10.
